metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students explore database design, networking fundamentals, information management, and cybersecurity basics. This course develops understanding of how data is organized, transmitted, protected, and used to support organizational operations.
metadata.general_description: An applied course in databases, networking, information management, and cybersecurity fundamentals.

big_ideas.statement: Data and information are strategic assets that require organized storage, secure management, and appropriate access controls.
big_ideas.elaboration: Database design and information management systems enable organizations to store, retrieve, and analyze data efficiently and securely.

big_ideas.statement: Computer networks connect devices and enable communication and resource sharing across organizations and globally.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding network fundamentals—protocols, devices, architectures—enables assessment of connectivity options and network performance.

big_ideas.statement: Cybersecurity requires multi-layered protection strategies to safeguard information against unauthorized access, modification, or loss.
big_ideas.elaboration: Security awareness and implementation of protective measures are responsibilities of all users in modern information systems.

big_ideas.statement: Information technology systems support organizational objectives while presenting ethical, privacy, and security considerations.
big_ideas.elaboration: Responsible information management balances organizational needs with protection of individual privacy and security.

competencies.raw: Design database structures to organize and manage organizational data
i_can_statements: I can analyze information needs and design database schemas
I can create entity-relationship diagrams for data relationships
I can normalize database designs to ensure efficiency
I can design databases for specific business applications

competencies.raw: Plan network architectures appropriate for organizational needs
i_can_statements: I can assess organizational network requirements
I can design network topologies for different contexts
I can plan network security architecture
I can develop network implementation plans

competencies.raw: Design information security and access control systems
i_can_statements: I can identify security threats and vulnerabilities
I can design access control systems and user permissions
I can plan data protection and encryption strategies
I can develop security policies and procedures

competencies.raw: Evaluate information systems for effectiveness and security
i_can_statements: I can assess database performance and efficiency
I can evaluate network functionality and security
I can identify system vulnerabilities and risks
I can recommend system improvements and upgrades

competencies.raw: Create and manage databases using database software
i_can_statements: I can create database tables with appropriate fields and data types
I can enter, retrieve, and modify data in databases
I can create queries to extract specific information
I can create reports and summaries from database data

competencies.raw: Operate and troubleshoot network devices and connections
i_can_statements: I can configure network devices and settings
I can troubleshoot connectivity issues
I can understand and apply network protocols
I can monitor network performance and traffic

competencies.raw: Apply information security practices and protocols
i_can_statements: I can implement authentication and access controls
I can apply encryption and data protection measures
I can create and manage user accounts and permissions
I can conduct security audits and assessments

competencies.raw: Manage information systems and user support
i_can_statements: I can provide technical support to users
I can diagnose and resolve common IT issues
I can maintain system documentation and records
I can manage backups and disaster recovery

competencies.raw: Use database management systems to organize and analyze data
i_can_statements: I can use relational database management systems
I can work with SQL and query languages
I can use data analysis and reporting tools
I can integrate databases with other applications

competencies.raw: Use networking tools to configure, monitor, and troubleshoot networks
i_can_statements: I can use network diagnostic and monitoring tools
I can configure and manage network devices
I can understand and apply network protocols
I can document and visualize network architectures

competencies.raw: Apply cybersecurity tools and practices to protect information
i_can_statements: I can use encryption and security software
I can configure firewalls and security appliances
I can conduct vulnerability scans and assessments
I can implement and monitor security controls

competencies.raw: Evaluate emerging technologies in data management and security
i_can_statements: I can assess cloud-based data storage and management
I can understand big data and analytics technologies
I can evaluate emerging security threats and solutions
I can identify new technologies relevant to IT systems

areas.name: Networking Fundamentals
topics: Network types and architectures: LAN, WAN, cloud

areas.name: Information Security and Privacy
topics: Privacy regulations and data protection laws

areas.name: Information Systems and Operations
topics: IT support and help desk functions
Ethical and legal considerations in IT
